summary: Well-implemented AI patch workflow with clean Preview/Apply/Undo lifecycle, multi-level parent gating, and transactional execution with reverse-order rollback. Also addresses 6/6 of my TASK-013 review findings (cache eviction, requires_approval in impact assessment, safety_check validation, context stripping, fingerprint type info, unknown-handler test). 1 medium + 4 low findings.

findings.title: undo() does not emit any domain event
findings.detail: apply() emits AIAssistanceAppliedEvent, but undo() only records in the action log without emitting a domain event. This creates an audit gap — event bus subscribers (including the audit ledger from TASK-019 and parent timeline views) will not see undo/revert actions. A reverted action should have visibility for safety auditing.
findings.recommendation: Emit a new domain event (e.g., AIAssistanceRevertedEvent) or reuse AIAssistanceAppliedEvent with a 'reverted' flag when an action is undone.

findings.title: APPROVED status is set then immediately overwritten to APPLIED
findings.detail: Line 80 sets action.status = APPROVED, then line 81 calls mark_applied() which overwrites to APPLIED. The APPROVED state is transient and never observable. If the intent is to record the transition through APPROVED, the action log at line 84 records previous_state as 'APPROVED' which is technically the state set 1 line above, not the real previous state (PROPOSED/PREVIEWING).
findings.recommendation: Either remove the explicit APPROVED assignment (rely on mark_applied only) or record the actual previous state. Current behavior is functionally correct but semantically misleading in the action log.

findings.title: preview() mutates action status as a side effect
findings.detail: preview() transitions PROPOSED -> PREVIEWING on first call. This makes preview a mutating operation rather than a pure read. Subsequent preview calls don't re-trigger (already PREVIEWING), so it's idempotent after first call, but callers expecting preview to be side-effect-free may be surprised.
findings.recommendation: Document this behavior. Alternatively, make PREVIEWING a separate explicit step if the workflow requires it.

findings.title: _actions dictionary is unbounded and in-memory only
findings.detail: Actions tracked via track_action() accumulate without limit and are not persisted. All tracked actions are lost on service restart. For MVP this is acceptable, but a long session with many AI interactions will accumulate stale actions.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a max_tracked_actions cap with eviction for completed/reverted/rejected actions, or a cleanup method.

findings.title: Action log records inaccurate previous_state for apply transition
findings.detail: The action log records previous_state as {status: 'APPROVED'} for the apply transition. But the action was PROPOSED or PREVIEWING before line 80 momentarily set it to APPROVED. The recorded 'previous' is technically the state set 1 line above, not the original status.
findings.recommendation: Capture the original status before mutation: var prev = _status_name(action.status) at the start of apply(), then use it in _record_state_change.

task_013_fixes_verified.unbounded_cache: FIXED — MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES=1000 with FIFO eviction via _cache_order
task_013_fixes_verified.safety_check_empty: FIXED — custom validation requires at least text or image_ref (line 105-112)
task_013_fixes_verified.default_execute_leaks_context: FIXED — _default_execute no longer includes context in result (line 133-138)
task_013_fixes_verified.unsupported_type_collision: FIXED — fingerprint now includes type ID: <unsupported:%d> (line 193)
task_013_fixes_verified.unknown_handler_test: FIXED — test added at line 56-59 in gateway contract test

positive_observations: Clean action-card lifecycle: PROPOSED -> PREVIEWING -> APPROVED -> APPLIED (or REVERTED/REJECTED)
Multi-level parent gating: action.needs_approval(), tool.requires_approval, hardcoded script_edit/asset_import
Transactional execution with reverse-order rollback matches TASK-012 pattern
Action log integration records both apply and undo state changes
Event emission on apply for audit trail
All 6 TASK-013 review findings addressed as part of this work
13 test assertions covering parent gating, kid blocking, apply, undo, and edge cases
Proper DI with optional dependencies (tool_gateway, action_log, event_bus)
